column1: # Class identity: battlefield rally-point and diplomat; makes allies braver; ends wars through voice
# Existing cards: 2 combat, 2 utility, 2 control, 1 power, 1 capstone = 8 total
# Gaps: L1 core (4 more to reach 12), L2 (2), L3 per path (4 each x 3 = 12), L5 (2 more to reach 3), L9 per path (2 each x 3 = 6). Capstone complete.

column3: Plant your banner and call the formation; until your next turn, allies within Command Zone cannot be pushed, pulled, or frightened.
Raise an open hand and speak the old battlefield word; one enemy who can hear and understand you must choose to disengage or accept your VEIL check before attacking.
Signal forward with voice and hand; one willing ally within Command Zone may move up to their speed as a reaction, ignoring opportunity attacks.
Your voice carries the authority of the banner; when an ally within earshot fails a RESONANCE or morale check, you may spend this card to let them reroll.
Plant your standard; while it stands, your Command Zone's radius increases and allies inside cannot be flanked.
You have learned the cadence that cuts through fear; once per combat, end one charmed, frightened, or compelled effect on yourself or an ally within earshot.

column1: # Battlefield rally-point. The banner stands, and so does the line.

column3: Shout the oldest oath of the banner; allies in Command Zone reduce incoming damage while you can see them and while you remain standing.
Strike and call a charge in the same motion; after your attack hits, each ally in Command Zone may shift one tile toward an enemy as a reaction.
So long as you hold the banner aloft, allies in sight of it add your VEIL bonus to their saves against fear and despair.
Begin the marching hymn of the old cohorts; each round it continues, allies who attack on their turn gain bravery, and enemies who hear it lose one die on morale checks.

column1: # Diplomat-warriors who end wars through voice. The tongue speaks all sides.

column3: Raise the neutral sign and name the terms; choose two enemies within earshot, they cannot attack each other or you until one of them is attacked again.
Speak aloud what the enemy truly wants; one humanoid enemy in earshot must make a RESONANCE check or lose their next attack action to argue with you.
Walk the line between the armies; until the start of your next turn, you cannot be the first target attacked by any enemy who has not already struck you this combat.
Offer quarter to a wounded foe; if a humanoid enemy is below half health and within earshot, they must choose to surrender, flee, or fight on with a penalty to their next attack.

column1: # Command-and-control. Orchestrates ally actions like a conductor.

column3: Flick a battlefield sign; one ally in Command Zone may immediately play a card from their hand at cost, using your initiative slot.
You see the engagement one beat ahead; until your next turn, the first attack each ally makes against your Priority Mark hits with advantage.
Fire the coded flare; every ally in sight learns the positions and health of every enemy you can see until the end of the round.
Call the rotation; two allies in Command Zone swap places, and each may make a free attack against a single enemy threatening either of their former positions.
Step onto high ground and speak as heralds spoke before the wars; until the start of your next turn, allies in earshot ignore fear and exhaustion and their next attack gains your VEIL bonus.
Invoke the oldest herald's oath between two hostile parties; for one round, no creature in Command Zone may make the first attack, and any who break the oath take resonance damage from the breaking.
So long as the banner stands and you can speak, allies in Command Zone who would drop to zero health instead remain at one until the end of the round.
Call the name of a cohort long dead; every ally in Command Zone moves up to their speed toward an enemy and makes a free attack, and the banner flies without a bearer for one round.
Speak the single syllable heralds are forbidden to teach; every humanoid enemy within earshot must make a RESONANCE save or drop their weapons and spend their next turn not attacking.

For one round, you speak in two voices at once; you may take a second reaction each round, and the first time an ally would be attacked by a humanoid enemy, you may redirect the attacker's action into a RESONANCE contest instead.

Conduct the firing line; every ally in Command Zone with a ranged attack fires at a single enemy as a reaction, and the Priority Mark on that enemy becomes permanent until they die or leave the field.
Until the end of your next turn, your allies act in the order you call; you set the initiative of every willing ally in Command Zone and they may interrupt each other's turns with reactions.
